input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS (iOS + Android):

---

## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | CLI compilers/linkers |
| **.NET SDK (latest)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | Runtime + CLI (`dotnet`) |
| **MAUI workload** | Via `dotnet` CLI | MAUI project templates & build targets |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically with Android workload (or [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)) | Android toolchain dependency |

---

## 2. Installation Steps

```bash
# 1. Install .NET SDK (use the installer from dot.net or Homebrew)
brew install --cask dotnet-sdk   # or use the official .pkg

# 2. Install the MAUI workload (pulls Android SDK, JDK, emulator)
dotnet workload install maui

# 3. Accept Xcode license (after installing Xcode from App Store)
sudo xcodebuild -license accept

# 4. Install Xcode Command Line Tools (if not already)
xcode-select --install
```

The `maui` workload bundles: `android`, `ios`, `maccatalyst`, and `maui-*` packs. It auto-provisions the Android SDK, NDK, build-tools, and emulator images.

---

## 3. Discovering the Right Versions

```bash
# See installed .NET SDKs
dotnet --list-sdks

# See available & installed workloads
dotnet workload list
dotnet workload search maui

# Check which Android SDK/JDK the workload installed
dotnet android sdk info          # or look in ~/Library/Android/sdk

# Check Xcode version & active path
xcodebuild -version
xcode-select -p
```

Compatibility matrix: [Microsoft's .NET MAUI support policy](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms) lists which Xcode, iOS SDK, and Android API levels each .NET version supports.

---

## 4. Verification

```bash
# Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet run -f net9.0-ios --project HelloMaui.csproj

# Run on Android Emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet run -f net9.0-android --project HelloMaui.csproj
```

If all four commands succeed, your environment is fully functional.

---

## 5. Optional but Recommended

- **Visual Studio Code** + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) — lightweight IDE with Hot Reload
- **Android Emulator** — create a device via `avdmanager` or from VS Code
- `dotnet workload update` — run periodically to stay current

---

**Key official docs:** [aka.ms/maui/getting-started](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation)
